title: [WeChall] Pm me to collaborate! [WeChall]->Challenge: The BrownOS

posts.author: macplox
posts.title: Pm me to collaborate!
posts.text: I’d like to share and discuss my findings with a partner or two. Message me if you’d like to team up with me and exchange our findings. The differences in our findings/theories/analyses should point us in the right direction.

posts.author: dloser
posts.title: New syscall enabled
posts.text:
Have you heard? There is a new version of BrownOS out!
It has a whole new syscall, but I haven't been able to figure out its purpose yet. Seems pretty useless so far...

posts.author: l3st3r
posts.title: RE: New syscall enabled
posts.text:
Quote from Gizmore
Sep 10, 2018 - 18:09:02
I still cant figure out that something might be the cmd to interrupt and transfer my parameters to the kernel
maybe someone can code something up?.
Click for spoiler
In Bash:
echo 00 01 02 03 ... | xxd -r -p | nc wc3.wechall.net 61221 | xxd
The spoiler being that the service takes binary input.  But that has been devulged elsewhere, so not much of a spoiler.
Hint: If you give it good input, you get good stuff back...  Now, what is good input? ;)

posts.author: dloser
posts.title: Some notes
posts.text:
The service has some restrictions on input size, memory and execution time. They should be more than fair for what you need, but if for some reason you think they are too restrictive, let me know and we'll have a look.
For those who are wondering what QD means, perhaps you should move your nose off the screen. :p It's right there, ffs! Some people make challenges out of anything and nothing... ;)
The only thing I'm not sure about is how hard it is to understand what is going on exactly. You should be able to deduce a few things, but perhaps it is not enough to power your light bulb. When you have deduced a fair number of things and can't make any more sense of it, feel free to contact me. If needed, I'll extend the cheat sheet a bit.

posts.author: dloser
posts.title: RE: Some notes
posts.text:
So.. two years and still no solvers. There are no words to express my disappointment in all of you. ;)
At least some of you have found a little bit of information, but I haven't heard of anyone figuring out the meaning of the input codes. Figuring out that part is probably the most important thing to do, if only because it is essential to eventually getting the solution. The outputs should help a lot in figuring this out, as well as just trying out various inputs.
Here are two little hints for those who want it:
Click for spoiler
The second example in the cheat sheet, besides providing a way to get some easy outputs, is also useful in figuring out some crucial properties of the codes.
Click for spoiler
The different outputs betray some core structures. This should give you some substructures that might be helpful elsewhere.
One final thing: just like with QD, don't be too literal with the ??s. :)

posts.author: dloser
posts.title: RE: Some notes
posts.text:
Just made a fix after jjk found some unusual behaviour. Normal inputs were fine, but certain inputs that didn't confirm to expectations resulted in odd behaviour.
I doubt this threw people off too much, but if it did you are eligible for one (1) apology.

posts.author: macplox
posts.title: RE: Some notes
posts.text:
I'm able to get the "Invalid term!" message if i echo anything with 0xFF bytes in it. Wireshark, amap and amapcrap were entertaining at best in terms of fuzzing it. Anyone try talking to the service with Scapy yet? I'm just starting to read up on it...
I'm curious if this has anything to do with things like eBPF

posts.author: dloser
posts.title: RE: Some notes
posts.text: The example from the cheat sheet should be a good start to not get that message...

posts.author: l3st3r
posts.title: RE: Some notes
posts.text:
I can make it return ...
"a towel!"
and
"O"
" towel!"
(two consecutive reads)
...using the exact same input data. Granted, I had to send a bad QD.
And yes, it is a binary protocol.
